jokes.text: "Gruesome twosome seek exotic Europeans for week long rental in luxurious one-bedroom apartment. Bed to be shared with owners. Shitter down hall. Email F. Reynolds at Warthog-OrgyFart.edu."
jokes.setup: Frank reads what should be a normal Airbnb listing
jokes.punchline: The increasingly inappropriate language: 'gruesome twosome,' 'shitter,' and the email 'Warthog-OrgyFart.edu'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank escalation - starts seemingly normal then gets progressively more disturbing. The email address is peak Frank absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Gruesome twosome seek exotic Europeans for week long rental in luxurious one-bedroom apartment. Bed to be shared with owners. Shitter down hall. Email F. Reynolds at Warthog-OrgyFart.edu.

jokes.text: "Young Bohemians with no sexual boundaries, w-who don't mind a shitter down the hall."
jokes.setup: Frank explaining their target demographic
jokes.punchline: The completely inappropriate way he describes potential guests
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank crudeness delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Young Bohemians with no sexual boundaries, w-who don't mind a shitter down the hall.

jokes.text: "Don't be jealous when Charlie and I are up to our eyebrows in Viennese gash."
jokes.setup: Frank boasting about their plan's success
jokes.punchline: The absurdly crude phrase 'up to our eyebrows in Viennese gash'
jokes.explanation: Peak Frank vulgarity with the visual of being 'up to eyebrows' - gross but memorable

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Don't be jealous when Charlie and I are up to our eyebrows in Viennese gash.

jokes.text: "Are you trying to say, uh, 'meat cube'? Like, a little cube of meat?"
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions 'meet-cute'
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete misunderstanding, visualizing actual meat cubes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his brain immediately goes to literal food interpretation of romantic concept

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Are you trying to say, uh, 'meat cube'? Like, a little cube of meat?

jokes.text: "Because that's a good idea, actually."
jokes.setup: Charlie mishears 'meet-cute' as 'meat cube'
jokes.punchline: He's genuinely excited about this food idea
jokes.explanation: Charlie's enthusiasm for what he thinks is a food concept shows his priorities

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Because that's a good idea, actually.

jokes.text: "Let-let's keep the trap simple."
jokes.setup: Dennis objects to calling their romantic plan a 'trap'
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately calls it a trap, missing Dennis's point entirely
jokes.explanation: Mac's inability to understand subtlety - he sees it as a trap and doesn't hide it

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Let-let's keep the trap simple.

jokes.text: "I must have overbooked... this Airbnb, and that's a problem... because we only have the one room"
jokes.setup: Mac tries to explain the booking conflict
jokes.punchline: His painfully obvious stalling and the absurd 'solution' of sharing rooms
jokes.explanation: Mac's terrible lying and improvisation under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I must have overbooked... this Airbnb, and that's a problem... because we only have the one room

jokes.text: "Now, d-do you like to come off as a difficult person?"
jokes.setup: Lisa says she doesn't want to be difficult
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately turns it around to manipulate her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis manipulation - taking her courtesy and weaponizing it

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now, d-do you like to come off as a difficult person?

jokes.text: Right. Yeah, but I was, uh, you know, I'm-I'm, but, you know, ch... Things change.
jokes.setup: Being caught in his contradiction about sharing rooms
jokes.punchline: Dennis's weak attempt to backtrack without admitting his creepy intentions
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pathetic attempt to save face while being obviously predatory is classic cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Right. Yeah, but I was, uh, you know, I'm-I'm, but, you know, ch... Things change.

jokes.text: "They're two Austrian broads. Alexi and Nikki."
jokes.setup: Frank describing their female guests
jokes.punchline: Setting up expectation for attractive women before the reveal
jokes.explanation: Setup for the gender reveal - the names could go either way but Frank assumes female

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: They're two Austrian broads. Alexi and Nikki.

jokes.text: Frank's detailed rules about avoiding eye contact and body exploration during group sex
jokes.setup: Frank laying out ground rules
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific fears about male-on-male contact during what he assumes will be an orgy
jokes.explanation: Frank's weirdly practical approach to what he thinks will be group sex

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank's detailed rules about avoiding eye contact and body exploration during group sex

jokes.text: And they're gonna encourage us to explore each other's bodies. - What? - A-And that's a hard no for me.
jokes.setup: Frank's previous sexual rule
jokes.punchline: Escalating to an even more disturbing scenario
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that gets even more disturbing, and Charlie's horror is perfectly played.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: And they're gonna encourage us to explore each other's bodies.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: A-And that's a hard no for me.

jokes.text: "Yeah. I am Nikki, and this is my father Alexi."
jokes.setup: Frank and Charlie expecting attractive female backpackers
jokes.punchline: Nikki is a young man and Alexi is his elderly father
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - gender assumptions completely subverted with father-son reveal

dialogue.speaker: Nikki
dialogue.line: Yeah. I am Nikki, and this is my father Alexi.

jokes.text: Oh, shit.
jokes.setup: Discovering their 'exotic Europeans' are two men
jokes.punchline: Frank's understated reaction to his plans being ruined
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that perfectly capture Frank's disappointment and the situation's absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, shit.

jokes.text: "I-I never put this much work into banging some cute meat."
jokes.setup: Mac complaining about Dennis's elaborate romantic plan
jokes.punchline: He's still using Charlie's 'meat cube' misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Mac has now adopted Charlie's confusion, spreading the misunderstanding

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I-I never put this much work into banging some cute meat.

jokes.text: "Its name is Lisa."
jokes.setup: Mac referring to Lisa as 'cute meat'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's overly romantic correction shows his delusional attachment
jokes.explanation: Dennis treating Lisa like a real love interest when she's just a target

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Its name is Lisa.

jokes.text: No, Dennis, 'meet-cute.' It has a name, Mac. Its name is Lisa.
jokes.setup: Mac's continued misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Dennis's pretentious correction and creepy personalization
jokes.explanation: Dennis treating a woman like a romantic comedy character is perfectly creepy and narcissistic for him.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, Dennis, 'meet-cute.' It has a name, Mac.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Its name is Lisa.

jokes.text: The third mysterious noise from the room
jokes.setup: They're listening to Lisa and Greg fight
jokes.punchline: Strange animal-like sounds that confuse them further
jokes.explanation: Adds mystery and confusion to their eavesdropping

dialogue.line: The third mysterious noise from the room

jokes.text: Dude, i-is that, like, a chicken or a parrot or some shit?
jokes.setup: Strange noises from the other room
jokes.punchline: Mac thinking the Austrian yodeling is animal sounds
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience also doesn't know what the sound is, and Mac's interpretation is perfectly ignorant.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dude, i-is that, like, a chicken or a parrot or some shit?

jokes.text: "That's no animal."
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis think they hear animal noises
jokes.punchline: Dee's ominous correction implies something worse
jokes.explanation: Dee's dramatic entrance with ominous implications

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That's no animal.

jokes.text: "Proving that romantic leads don't have to be likable at all."
jokes.setup: Dennis casting Dee as the new romantic lead
jokes.punchline: His backhanded 'compliment' about Dee not needing to be likable
jokes.explanation: Dennis insulting Dee while pretending to cast her - typical sibling cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Proving that romantic leads don't have to be likable at all.

jokes.text: Uh, may I ask if you have a small pot that I can boil these lederhosen in?
jokes.setup: Normal apartment guest request
jokes.punchline: The completely bizarre request to boil leather pants
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd request delivered matter-of-factly. The image of boiling lederhosen is wonderfully ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Nikki
dialogue.line: Uh, may I ask if you have a small pot that I can boil these lederhosen in?

jokes.text: What do you got going there with the toe?
jokes.setup: Observing Alexi
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual interest in what's clearly disgusting toe maintenance
jokes.explanation: Charlie's disgusting curiosity is perfectly in character. Sets up the toe spoon running gag.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What do you got going there with the toe?

jokes.text: "'Toe spoon,' for the digging out of the toe."
jokes.setup: Charlie asking what Alexi is doing to his toe
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact explanation of the disgusting 'toe spoon'
jokes.explanation: Memorably gross concept delivered with casual European practicality

dialogue.line: 'Toe spoon,' for the digging out of the toe.

jokes.text: "We usually sleep ass to ass."
jokes.setup: Discussing sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact description of their weird sleeping position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - completely inappropriate oversharing delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We usually sleep ass to ass.

jokes.text: "I came at this thing like an animal, man."
jokes.setup: Dennis describing his toilet destruction
jokes.punchline: His pride in the intensity of his vandalism
jokes.explanation: Dennis's twisted pride in his gross sabotage

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I came at this thing like an animal, man.

jokes.text: "It's not mine."
jokes.setup: Greg discovers the destroyed toilet
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact denial of responsibility for the mess
jokes.explanation: Innocent victim's reasonable response to inexplicable situation

dialogue.speaker: Greg
dialogue.line: It's not mine.

jokes.text: I mean, Dennis is having meet cubes with hot chicks, and we're stuck with these guys.
jokes.setup: Charlie feeling left out
jokes.punchline: Still misunderstanding 'meet-cute' while being oblivious to his good fortune
jokes.explanation: Good callback with dramatic irony - Charlie doesn't realize he's having the better time.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I mean, Dennis is having meet cubes with hot chicks, and we're stuck with these guys.

jokes.text: But the trick is to melt the cheese on the outside.
jokes.setup: Describing his sandwich-making technique
jokes.punchline: The bizarre food preparation method
jokes.explanation: Absurdist food preparation that sounds disgusting but is delivered with complete sincerity.

dialogue.line: But the trick is to melt the cheese on the outside.

jokes.text: I've always kind of seen myself as a yodeler.
jokes.setup: Charlie wanting to learn from Nikki
jokes.punchline: His random aspiration to be a yodeler
jokes.explanation: Charlie's completely random career aspiration is perfectly in character for his scattered mind.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I've always kind of seen myself as a yodeler.

jokes.text: Frank and Charlie immediately kicking out the European women they wanted
jokes.setup: They finally get what they originally wanted
jokes.punchline: They reject it because they've bonded with Alexi and Nikki
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - they choose the 'wrong' guests they've grown to love

dialogue.line: Frank and Charlie immediately kicking out the European women they wanted

jokes.text: Sorry, no. We double-booked.
jokes.setup: Two attractive European women arriving
jokes.punchline: Frank and Charlie turning them away for the men they bonded with
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - they turn away exactly what they thought they wanted for unexpected genuine connection.

dialogue.line: Sorry, no. We double-booked.

jokes.text: "You know, I can always use a man to help me clean my pipes."
jokes.setup: Dee trying to be seductive about the plunger
jokes.punchline: Her terrible sexual innuendo about plumbing
jokes.explanation: Dee's awkward attempt at seduction with plumbing double entendre

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You know, I can always use a man to help me clean my pipes.

jokes.text: "Help flushing the shit you took that backed up your toilet?"
jokes.setup: Dee's sexual innuendo about pipes
jokes.punchline: Greg's blunt, literal interpretation kills the mood
jokes.explanation: Perfect mood killer - Greg's practical response destroys Dee's seduction attempt

dialogue.line: Help flushing the shit you took that backed up your toilet?

jokes.text: "End act two."
jokes.setup: Greg walking away from Dee
jokes.punchline: Dennis narrating his romantic comedy like it's actually happening
jokes.explanation: Dennis's delusion reaching meta-commentary levels - he thinks he's directing a movie

dialogue.line: End act two.

jokes.text: "Dig that guy up, you know, see what he's been up to these days."
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to be understanding about Lisa missing Teddy
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting necrophilia without realizing Teddy is dead
jokes.explanation: Accidentally dark humor - Dennis unknowingly suggests grave robbing while trying to be accommodating

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dig that guy up, you know, see what he's been up to these days.

jokes.text: "I thought the crying was hot."
jokes.setup: Mac questioning how Dennis thinks that went well
jokes.punchline: Dennis's disturbing attraction to emotional distress
jokes.explanation: Reveals Dennis's predatory nature - he's aroused by vulnerability and pain

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I thought the crying was hot.

jokes.text: I thought the crying was hot.
jokes.setup: Lisa crying and leaving
jokes.punchline: Mac's disturbing interpretation of her grief as sexual
jokes.explanation: Mac's disturbing sexual response to genuine grief is perfectly awful and in character for his complete lack of empathy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I thought the crying was hot.

jokes.text: But that's because the guy in 4B went berserk and ripped it off.
jokes.setup: Explaining why there's no bathroom door
jokes.punchline: The completely over-the-top explanation for a missing door
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual mention of apartment violence to explain a basic amenity missing is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: But that's because the guy in 4B went berserk and ripped it off.

jokes.text: The description of Charlie and Frank's trashy apartment
jokes.setup: Showing their apartment to European guests
jokes.punchline: The bathroom has no door and they describe it as 'bohemian'
jokes.explanation: Frank and Charlie's delusional presentation of squalor as European sophistication

jokes.text: I do that for Frank when he's taking a dump in there.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his door service
jokes.punchline: The gross specificity of what he does for Frank
jokes.explanation: Charlie's matter-of-fact description of enabling Frank's bathroom privacy is perfectly gross and codependent.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I do that for Frank when he's taking a dump in there.

jokes.text: "I can become the door for you."
jokes.setup: Explaining the doorless bathroom situation
jokes.punchline: Charlie's offer to physically stand as a human door
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie absurdity - treating himself as furniture with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I can become the door for you.

jokes.text: "You are a diversionary plot twist at best."
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining Dee's role in his romantic comedy
jokes.punchline: Reducing Dee to a minor plot device while insulting her
jokes.explanation: Dennis's meta-fictional insult perfectly captures his view of others as supporting characters

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You are a diversionary plot twist at best.

jokes.text: I do want some of those meat cubes later.
jokes.setup: Frank commenting on the 'romance'
jokes.punchline: Frank also misunderstanding meet-cute as meat cubes
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the misunderstanding has spread to Frank too. His food obsession is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I do want some of those meat cubes later.

jokes.text: "Maybe Teddy cheated with Greg."
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to figure out the Teddy situation
jokes.punchline: His assumption that it must be a gay affair because he can't conceive of other explanations
jokes.explanation: Dennis's mind immediately goes to sexual explanations for any emotional situation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Maybe Teddy cheated with Greg.

jokes.text: "We'll jam it down their throats till they enjoy it."
jokes.setup: Dennis acknowledging the gay romance won't play well
jokes.punchline: His aggressive plan to force acceptance
jokes.explanation: Dennis's authoritarian approach to artistic acceptance - very in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We'll jam it down their throats till they enjoy it.

jokes.text: "Men are not in charge anymore."
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis planning to control her romantic life
jokes.punchline: Dee's feminist protest that immediately gets ignored
jokes.explanation: Dee's valid point that gets immediately dismissed by the male characters

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Men are not in charge anymore.

jokes.text: "I saw them using Alexi's toe spoon to cook heroin."
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining why something feels wrong
jokes.punchline: The disgusting toe spoon being repurposed for drug use
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that makes the toe spoon even more disgusting by adding drug use

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I saw them using Alexi's toe spoon to cook heroin.

jokes.text: Frank and Charlie realizing they miss Alexi and Nikki
jokes.setup: They got exactly what they originally wanted
jokes.punchline: They prefer the 'wrong' guests they bonded with
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing they formed genuine connections despite the gross circumstances

dialogue.line: Frank and Charlie realizing they miss Alexi and Nikki

jokes.text: "A gay... gay-ass love story."
jokes.setup: Mac trying to be romantic and progressive
jokes.punchline: His clumsy, potentially offensive phrasing
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt at progressive language coming out wrong and potentially offensive

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: A gay... gay-ass love story.

jokes.text: "I dumped up your toilet, Greg."
jokes.setup: Mac's romantic confession
jokes.punchline: His confession of the gross toilet sabotage as a romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Mac confessing to Dennis's crime as if it's a romantic gesture - peak absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I dumped up your toilet, Greg.

jokes.text: "Teddy was our son. He died of leukemia."
jokes.setup: All the speculation about who Teddy was and romantic triangles
jokes.punchline: The devastating reveal that Teddy was their dead child
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - completely recontextualizes all previous assumptions about Teddy with devastating simplicity

dialogue.line: Teddy was our son. He died of leukemia.

jokes.text: The immediate awkward silence and everyone's reaction to learning about the dead child
jokes.setup: The shocking revelation about Teddy
jokes.punchline: Complete speechlessness from the gang who realize they've been horrible
jokes.explanation: The moment where even these terrible people realize they've gone too far

jokes.text: "I don't have a dead kid's... uh, approp-appropriate..."
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to backtrack from the dead child revelation
jokes.punchline: His inability to form appropriate words while still being self-centered
jokes.explanation: Dennis still making it about himself even while trying to show sensitivity

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't have a dead kid's... uh, approp-appropriate...

jokes.text: That's not romantic. Or comedic.
jokes.setup: The dead child revelation ruining their romantic comedy
jokes.punchline: Dennis's matter-of-fact assessment of the genre failure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the show itself. Dennis's clinical assessment of their failed rom-com is perfect self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's not romantic. Or comedic.

jokes.text: "That's not romantic. Or comedic."
jokes.setup: The tragic dead child revelation
jokes.punchline: Mac's assessment of how it fits into their romantic comedy framework
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on how their delusional romantic comedy crashed into tragic reality

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's not romantic. Or comedic.

jokes.text: Frank and Charlie desperately calling for Alexi and Nikki at the bus station
jokes.setup: They realized they miss their original guests
jokes.punchline: Their frantic search shows genuine emotional attachment
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly touching moment showing Frank and Charlie capable of genuine emotion

dialogue.line: Frank and Charlie desperately calling for Alexi and Nikki at the bus station

jokes.text: "We will spend our days together, knee-deep in American gash."
jokes.setup: Alexi's romantic farewell promise
jokes.punchline: Using Frank's crude phrase in a heartfelt romantic context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms Frank's crude phrase into a genuinely romantic promise

dialogue.speaker: Alexi
dialogue.line: We will spend our days together, knee-deep in American gash.

jokes.text: "He had me at 'gash,' Charlie."
jokes.setup: Frank's emotional response to Alexi's promise
jokes.punchline: Parody of romantic movie clichés using the crudest possible word
jokes.explanation: Perfect romantic comedy parody using the most inappropriate word possible - quintessential Frank

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: He had me at 'gash,' Charlie.
